basicInfo.name: Youngjin Jang (Vincent)
basicInfo.title: Backend Engineer · Tech Lead
basicInfo.tagline: Rather than a developer who shines alone, I aspire to be one who — like the moon — glows in harmony with the light of others.
basicInfo.introduction: Backend engineer with 5+ years of experience leading core systems at early-stage startups across Web2 SaaS and Web3. My sweet spot is the Zero-to-One phase — turning a vague product idea into a production-grade backend that scales to enterprise customers and carries the next funding round. I love building tools that close the gap between emerging technology and the way teams actually work.

AIM Intelligence.summary: Solely designed and shipped v1.0 of two products — Starfort (admin control plane) and Bastion-Guardian (sub-20ms GenAI DLP gateway) — at ~95% authorship across 668 commits in 6 months. Directly contributed to closing the company's Series A. Adopted by Korean enterprise partners across telecom and finance (LG, Hana, Kyobo, etc.) with both SaaS and On-Premise deployment.

projects.name: Starfort — Admin Control Plane
projects.description: Designed an AWS IAM-inspired Policy Engine combining Role hierarchy (Company → Org → Project) auto-gating with IAM-style fine-grained policies in a 2-stage evaluation model. Built a PostgreSQL-based Transactional Outbox for at-least-once dual-write consistency with external Langfuse — without adding Redis/SQS. Implemented 3-tier in-memory cache with single-flight pattern and cross-instance invalidation for Bastion-Guardian's hot path. Designed a read/write-domain-separated monorepo (admin + bridge) with EKS Helm and credential-less CI/CD via GitHub Actions OIDC.

projects.name: Bastion-Guardian — GenAI DLP Gateway
projects.description: Sub-20ms hot-path latency through layered in-memory cache and lightweight pre-check branching. Adopted Bun runtime + single-binary + distroless image for fast cold starts and minimal attack surface. Designed a 14-step parsing DSL and built a from-scratch jq mini-interpreter (lexer / parser / AST cache / evaluator) — zero jq/WASM deps — to handle heterogeneous AI service protocols (ChatGPT REST JSON, M365 Copilot SignalR WebSocket, Claude, multipart, base64). Provides an OpenAI-compatible gateway (drop-in API key) and a JWT + Serial Key Desktop Agent flow with silent recovery for unassigned states.

Waifu Sweeper.summary: Web3 wallet-based Minesweeper RPG built on Abstract zk-rollup with NFT/SBT/Shop contracts; collaborated with YGG (Yield Guild Games) on Partner API. Solo authorship of 60/61 backend commits. 10K+ DAU and 100M+ KRW cumulative payments.

projects.name: Wallet Auth (EIP-1271)
projects.description: Full-stack wallet authentication compatible with smart-contract wallets (AA Wallet / Abstract Global Wallet) using EIP-1271 magic-value verification. Mitigated concurrent-login and zombie-account issues with token hash storage; enforced single algorithm at JWT verify to block algorithm confusion.

projects.name: On-chain Payment Verification (4-gate)
projects.description: Cross-validated receipt logs across sender / recipient / price / packageId; normalized txHash to keep duplicate credit at zero. Implemented EIP-712 typed-data payment signer with one-time-use nonces.

projects.name: Postgres-based Distributed Lock
projects.description: Self-built distributed lock via Postgres unique constraints + TTL-based release — applied to game actions, payments, and cron leader elections. Revert flows wrap validation + update in a single transaction to avoid TOCTOU.

projects.name: YGG Partner API + Quest Pipeline
projects.description: Bidirectional HMAC signing + 30s dedup distributed lock for outsourced partner traffic security. EventEmitter-based fan-out for ~30 quest-clear callbacks; race-safe revert transactions for points reconciliation.

projects.name: AWS Terraform IaC
projects.description: Self-built AWS infrastructure: multi-AZ VPC, RDS, ALB, ECS, S3, CloudFront, ACM. Credential-less CI/CD via GitHub Actions OIDC.

Gomble.summary: Tech Lead handling 4–60K req/sec at TGE events with 10K+ DAU. Established the team's server template and engineering conventions, transitioned the platform's HTTP engine from Express to Fastify (~15% per-core throughput gain), built minigame integration with key-based encryption, and introduced Redash to shorten event-decision lead times.

projects.name: Gomble Squad
projects.description: Implemented large-scale Launchpool #3 event; introduced Redash for business-level data visibility and analytics; refactored / migrated to the team server template.

projects.name: Integrated Mini Game Server
projects.description: Unified server for multiple minigames within Gomble Squad. Established the team's backend template + coding conventions. Added per-user encryption keys + integrity verification to secure the boundary between in-game currency and on-chain assets.

projects.name: TGE Server
projects.description: Handled 4–60K req/sec traffic during the token generation event. Switched HTTP engine to Fastify for ~15% per-core gain. Indexed Web3 assets internally for accurate event data.

projects.name: Gomble Builders
projects.description: Developed an NFT-based voting system to select in-game characters. Implemented NFT indexing and optimized infrastructure costs via ECR/ECS.

projects.name: Mew Survivor
projects.description: External game server with Google/Apple OAuth login and IAP logic.

CatzeLabs.summary: Backend engineer on Yooldo, the company's P2E game publishing platform. Designed the deposit pipeline (Dual Deposit Finality / DDF) that cut on-chain settlement from 15 minutes to 15 seconds (60×). Built the company's unified backoffice (Slack OTP / QR 2FA / RBAC, gRPC server-to-server). Authored two internal libraries (Logger / Crypto). 4× Web3 hackathon wins (KlayMakers22, BNB Hackathon, Coinbase Buidl The Future, Aptos Seoul Hack 2023) — directly contributing to the company's ConsenSys investment.

projects.name: Yooldo — P2E Publishing Platform
projects.description: Full backend for an NFT-based P2E platform serving 900K+ users. Implemented Web3 indexers, real-time token ↔ in-game currency conversions, the DDF deposit pipeline (15min → 15s, 60×), Google OAuth, and an anti-abuse judicial system.

projects.name: Catze Backoffice
projects.description: Company-wide internal management system — Google OAuth, Slack OTP, QR 2FA, RBAC, gRPC for server-to-server communication. Reduced engineering load by enabling non-engineering staff to operate multi-product workflows.

projects.name: Catze Nest Logger
projects.description: Decorator-based NestJS logger enabling automatic method tracing and log injection; established company-wide logging standards. Co-built dashboards on Loki + Prometheus + Grafana.

projects.name: Catze Nest Crypto
projects.description: Envelope-encryption library (RSA + AES-256-CBC) provided as endpoint-level decorators; used across Catze internal services for secure payload handling.

projects.name: R3plica
projects.description: Hackathon project — 2nd at BNB Hackathon, 1st at Coinbase Buidl The Future. Built backend APIs, schemas, and Web3 indexers.

projects.name: Aptoplay & aptoplay-core
projects.description: Aptos Seoul Hack 2023 winner. Built the full backend and published aptoplay-core library to npm.

projects.name: KUP
projects.description: KlayMakers22 — 1st in track. Initial planning and architecture discussions for an NFT/Metaverse hackathon project.

projects.name: Alice-bot
projects.description: Discord bot for CyberGalz — virtual currency economy + Twitter-based content filters; cross-product integration.

projects.name: Trouble Punk Game Server
projects.description: P2E game server using Firebase Firestore — league system + reward settlement.

projects.name: Brickgirls
projects.description: Telegram Web3 game publishing platform — DB schema, APIs, Telegram Bot integration.

VSQUARE.summary: Full-stack development of CMS/LMS solutions across 7 simultaneous client projects. Drove engineering efficiency through self-learning and documentation culture, contributing to the company hitting prior-year revenue within two quarters.

projects.name: Multi-Project Full-Stack Delivery
projects.description: Delivered BE/FE for 7 client projects: Jaseng Korean Medicine Hospital LMS, Childcare Meal Management Support Center, Korea Women's Human Rights Institute LMS, Jeonghwa Arts University, Chongshin University, and Project Cygnus (E-commerce).

projects.name: Highlights
projects.description: Bulk email dispatch, certificate PDF/Excel export (Apache POI), drag-and-drop meal planner with auto nutrient calculation, and a multi-PG payment system integrating Inicis + PayPal for global payments.

personalProjects.name: Coagent (CLI + Desktop App)
personalProjects.description: Multi-agent chat — humans and multiple Claude Code agents collaborate in the same room via @mentions. CLI is the OSS core; Desktop App (Electron + Svelte 5) is the end-user product. Solves the Claude Agent SDK plain-text drop problem by enforcing a single MCP send_chat tool exit, and stabilizes SDK CLI spawn inside packaged GUIs via RunAsNode fuse + asar unpack + macOS Finder PATH recovery. In active use across my workplaces.
features: 9 slash control ops (clear / compact / usage / mode / model / pause / resume / kill, etc.)

personalProjects.name: Moonshine
personalProjects.description: Local-first knowledge graph desktop app — capture-only flow that auto-distills via embeddings + relation extraction in the background, visualizing the graph with Cytoscape.js. Server-zero, BYO API key. Built solo for macOS / Windows / Linux on a single Tauri + Svelte + Rust codebase. Distinguishing detail: a deadlock-free pattern interleaving sync SQLite locks with async AI calls (await-time mutex drop).
features: Wayland DBus workaround for global shortcut parity
Homebrew Cask + GitHub Releases + Tauri auto-updater

personalProjects.name: Zettelkasten-CLI
personalProjects.description: Terminal-native Zettelkasten knowledge management — Bun + TypeScript with 226 test cases across 12 releases. Models notes in 3 tiers (Fleeting → Zettel ← Literature) with promote-time auto-assignment of Luhmann-style alphanumeric IDs. Foreign-key policy preserves graph integrity across ID changes. Single-binary distribution with multi-step Web UI asset resolver works identically across Homebrew, curl install, and dev mode.
features: Reason-tagged links (support / refute / extend / contrast / question)

awards.title: BNB Hackathon Track #1: The Road towards Decentralized Society
Coinbase Buidl The Future Track — Open Ideas

education.school: Seoul National University of Science and Technology
